question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics showing a 0.3% monthly increase, which is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. The market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty since the official data is already available and shows 0.3% is well below the 0.9% requirement.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased 0.3% monthly
0.3% is substantially below the 0.9% threshold needed for YES
Data has been officially released and confirmed by multiple sources
Market closes April 10th, same day as CPI release
risks: Potential data revisions (though highly unlikely to change 0.3% to >0.9%)
Misunderstanding of resolution criteria (though clearly stated as >0.9%)
